name: Show HN (Hacker News)
description: Show HN is the Hacker News section for sharing something you built. The audience is technical (engineers, founders, hackers) and rewards substance over marketing.

auth.notes: Hacker News username + password account (no email-only field; the login and signup forms live on the same page). New accounts may not be able to submit immediately and outbound links are nofollow.

best_practices: Title must start with 'Show HN:' and describe what it actually does, e.g. 'Show HN: Acme – open-source <thing> that <does X>'. Avoid hype words like 'revolutionary' or 'best'.
Be technically honest: say what works, what doesn't, the stack, and any known limitations — the crowd punishes overselling and rewards candor.
Link directly to the working thing (live demo, repo, or app), not a marketing splash page with an email gate.
Write a short first comment (the 'text' field) explaining why you built it, how it works, and what feedback you want — this seeds the discussion.
Post in US morning hours (around 8-10am ET on a weekday) for maximum visibility on the front page.
Stick around and reply to every comment quickly and substantively; engaged authors get far more traction.
Don't ask for upvotes or use any growth-hacking on HN — it gets flagged and can hurt your account.

questions.label: Title
questions.help: Must begin with 'Show HN:'. Format: 'Show HN: <Product> – <what it does>'. No marketing speak.

questions.label: URL
questions.help: Link to the live product, demo, or repo. Either URL or text is required.

questions.label: Text (first comment)
questions.help: Background: why you built it, how it works, the stack, and what feedback you'd like. Keep it concise and honest.
